series_metadata.series_description: Audits and reviews performed by or on behalf of an agency, including the working papers that support the audit. Also includes audits performed on the agency.

tracking_data.comments:
The State Auditor’s Office retains
any copies of its audits performed
on Texas state agencies.

series_metadata.series_title: Biennial Budget Requests / Legislative Appropriation Requests (LARs)
series_metadata.series_description: Including any supporting documentation created and/or used to justify and support legislative appropriations requests by an agency.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival
requirement is met by sending the
required copies of the requests to
the University Archivist. Only copies
of supporting documentation
submitted to the Legislative Budget
Board are archival.

series_metadata.series_description: From employees and others concerning the institution and records pertaining to the resolution of the complaint.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes
the subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of item
number 1.1.048.

series_metadata.series_description: Incoming/outgoing and internal correspondence/email, in any media, pertaining to or arising from the routine operations of the policies, programs, services, or projects of an institution.

tracking_data.comments:
SEE comment to item number
1.1.007. SEE ALSO item number
1.1.010.

series_metadata.series_description: Any document that officially initiates, rescinds, or amends general office procedures.
Any document that initiates, rescinds, or amends a regulation, policy, or procedure that governs the programs, services, or projects of an institution.

Calendars, appointment books or programs and scheduling, or itinerary records, purchased with state funds or maintained by staff during business hours that document appointments, itineraries, and other activities of agency officials or employees.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected
officials, executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
University Archivist when these
records have met their retention
periods. CAUTION: A record of this
type purchased with personal
funds, but used by a state official to
document his or her work activities
may be a state record and subject
to this retention period. See Open
Records Decision 635 issued in
December 1995 by the Attorney
General.

series_metadata.series_description: From agency legal counsel or the Attorney General, including any requests eliciting the opinions

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include legal
opinions or advice rendered on a
matter in litigation or with regard
to pending litigation. SEE item
number 1.1.048.

series_metadata.series_description: News, press releases, or any public relations files maintained or issued by an agency. Includes print, electronic, audio, and audiovisual records.
Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are furnished to the public under Public Information Act (Chapter 552, Government Code).
Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are exempt under the Public Information Act (Chapter 552, Government Code).

Plans and records relating to the process of planning new or redefined programs, services or projects of an institution that are not included in or directly related to other records series in this schedule.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_metadata.series_description: Reports, Studies & Analyses

series_metadata.series_title: Long- and Short-Range Data Processing Plans

series_metadata.series_description: Copies of all proposed, withdrawn, emergency, and adopted rules; open meeting notices; or any other documents required by law to be submitted to the Texas Register.
Drafts of proposed legislation and related correspondence.

series_metadata.series_title: Reports, Studies, and Surveys - Final (non- routine or special) / Customer Surveys
series_metadata.series_description: Surveys returned by the customer or clients of an agency, and the statistical data maintained rating an agency’s performance.

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 1.1.067 for
summary reports compiled from
customer surveys.

series_metadata.series_description: Notes or text of speeches, papers or presentations delivered in conjunction with agency work.
Suggestions submitted by institution personnel and responses.
Instructional materials developed by an institution for training entities or individuals it regulates or serves.

Records created by or on behalf of an institution in anticipation of or in the adjudication of a lawsuit. (Also includes files/records related to formal complaints filed with the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) of the U.S. Department of Labor.)

tracking_data.comments:
All statutes of limitations must have
been met before file is closed.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
University Archivist for archival
preservation.

series_metadata.series_description: Logs or similar records used to register persons appearing before state agencies as required by Chapter 2004, Government Code, including quarterly reports filed with the Texas Ethics Commission
series_metadata.legal_citation: Government Code, including quarterly reports filed with the Texas Ethics Commission

series_metadata.series_description: Includes information resources and operational strategic plans prepared in accordance with Tex. Govt. Code, §§ 2054.095 and 2056.002.

tracking_data.comments:
UTSA retains the permanent
Record Copy and sends required
copies to the University Archivist.

series_metadata.series_description: Self evaluations and plans documenting compliance with Americans With Disabilities Act.
Official agenda and minutes of state boards/committees, commissions, and councils that conduct open meetings as required by Government Code, Chapter 551.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTES: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement will be met by
sending a copy to the University
Archivist. CAUTION: This records
series and item number 1.1.059,
1.1.060, 1.1.061 and 1.1.062 must
be used for those state boards,
committees, commissions, and
councils, which by law or the
biennial Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency.

series_metadata.series_title: Meetings, Certified Agendas or Tape Recordings of Closed
series_metadata.series_description: Certified agendas or tape recordings of closed meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils.

tracking_data.comments:
SEE caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_metadata.series_title: Meetings, Audio or Videotapes of Open
series_metadata.series_description: Audio or videotapes of open meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, etc.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Minutes of state
agencies are permanent records.
Audio and videotapes are not
permanent media. State agencies
may not retain audio or videotape
of the meetings of governing
bodies in lieu of written minutes.
The proceedings of all meetings of
state boards, committees,
commissions, and councils must be
reduced to writing. SEE caution
comment at item number 1.1.058.

series_metadata.series_description: Notes taken during open meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils from which written minutes are prepared.

tracking_data.comments:
SEE caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_metadata.series_description: Documents submitted at meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils including exhibit items, documentation for agenda items, etc. Includes documents sent in advance of meetings for briefing purposes, some of which may not be submitted at an actual meeting.

tracking_data.comments:
SEE caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_metadata.series_description: Minutes or notes, and supporting documentation, taken at internal staff meetings.

Any records of an agency needed for the documentation of output, outcome, efficiency, and explanatory measures in an agency’s appropriations request or strategic plan, and for performance measures used to manage the agency.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: The FE+3 retention
period overrides any shorter
retention period for a records
series in this schedule if the records
series is needed for documentation
of agency performance measures.

series_metadata.series_title: Reports, Studies, and Surveys - Raw Data
series_metadata.series_description: Information or data collected and compiled for the purpose of producing non-fiscal reports.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for
information or data included in or
directly related to another records
series in this schedule. SEE
especially item number 1.1.064.

series_metadata.series_title: Reports - Annual and Biennial Agency - Record Copy
series_metadata.series_description: Biennial and Annual narrative reports to the governor and legislature as required.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival
requirement is met by sending the
required copies of the reports to
the University Archivist.

series_metadata.series_title: Reports - Administrative or By Consultants or Committees (not including fiscal records)
series_metadata.series_description: Annual, sub-annual, or special reports or studies on non-fiscal aspects of an agency’s programs, services, or projects compiled by agency personnel, by advisory committees, or by consultants under contract with an agency that are not noted elsewhere in this schedule. Includes Energy Management Reports and other reports distributed either internally or to other entities.
Quarterly and annual reports on institution performance measures submitted to the executive and legislative offices.

tracking_data.comments:
UTSA retains the permanent
Record Copy.

series_metadata.series_title: Reports - Employee (activity and production type reports)
series_metadata.series_description: Reports compiled by agency personnel on a daily or other periodic basis pertaining to workload monitoring, task completion times, number of public contacts, and similar activities.

tracking_data.comments: CAUTION: See item number 1.1.064

series_metadata.series_title: Agency Rules, Policies, and Procedures - Final
series_metadata.series_description: Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules, or similar records distributed internally for the use of employees or externally to the pubic or those individuals or entities regulated by an agency that sets out the rules, policies, and procedures that govern an agency's programs, services or projects.

tracking_data.comments: SEE ALSO: Item number 1.1.071

series_metadata.series_title: Agency Rules, Policies, and Procedures - Working Files
series_metadata.series_description: Working files used in the development of manuals, guidelines, administrative rules, or similar records distributed internally for the use of employees or externally for the public or for those individuals or entities regulated by an agency that sets out the rules, policies, and procedures that govern and agencies and programs, services or projects

tracking_data.comments: SEE ALSO: item number 1.1.070

series_metadata.series_description: Transcripts and final decisions of hearings conducted as part of the regulatory process, and hearings on proposed rules and changes. The records may be maintained with related information including meeting notices, proofs of publication, and meeting minutes.

Final agreement described by Government Code Chapter 2009.054(c), associated with a matter conducted under an alternative dispute resolution procedure in which personnel of a state agency participated as a party on the agency's behalf.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice
and Remedies Code,
Chapter 154.071.

series_metadata.series_title: Deeds and Other Legal Documents Reflecting Legal Title
series_metadata.series_description: Property deeds, appraisals, surveys, description of property and all other supporting documents.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: documents of ownership of
vehicles are retained per 5.6.007.

series_metadata.series_description: Not related to litigation

tracking_data.comments:
Note: If correspondence relates to
a matter in litigation, see 1.1.048.

series_metadata.series_description: Any documents relating to a patent or trademark.
Any documents relating to a copyright (whether officially registered with the US Patent and Trademark Office or not).
Possible claim under Texas Tort Claims Act

tracking_data.comments:
Note: If matter leads to litigation
see 1.1.048.

CAUTION: Grants and sponsors may
have individuals guidelines for
retention. Records must be
retained in accordance with
guidelines of grant or contract.

series_metadata.series_description: Legal documents requiring recipient to appear in court to testify, or to produce records to be used in litigation.
Executed (signed) release/indemnity forms.
Agency level documents authorizing final disposition of records under a certified records retention schedule.
Print masters of original version and all subsequent revisions to an institution form, including any associated design or design modification requests.
Any periodic listing of all forms used internally or externally by an agency.
UTSA copy of SLR 105. Includes documentation of certification and approval - forms SLR 105C, and/or other forms designated by the State Records Administrator.

tracking_data.comments:
Original is retained by Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_metadata.series_description: Forms indicate records transferred to storage or a transfer of legal custody.

series_metadata.series_title: Request for Authority to Dispose of State Records (RMD 102)
series_metadata.series_description: UTSA Copy

tracking_data.comments:
Original is retained by SLRMD of
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission.

series_metadata.series_description: Logs or similar records listing records destroyed or transferred to the University Archivist, showing records series title, dates of records, and date destroyed or transferred.
UTSA copy.
Includes indexes, card files, shelf lists, registers, guides, etc.
Records management plans and similar records that establish the policies and procedures under which records and information are managed in an agency.

series_metadata.series_title: Institution Publications - Record Copy (Not University Catalogs)
series_metadata.series_description: One copy of each institution publication except a publication that is subject to a different retention period in this schedule.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, by law
Government Code 441.101-
441.106). The Texas State Library
and Archives Commission will
retain a copy of all state
publications submitted to it on a
continuing basis, subject to periodic
evaluation to determine if the
publication merits further
retention. SEE 6.4.108 for
University Catalogs.

series_metadata.series_description: Background material, copy (drafts), original artwork, photo negatives, prints, flats, etc. This includes all work performed both inside and outside the institution.

tracking_data.comments:
May be transferred to the
University Archivist after 10 years
but must be maintained
permanently.

series_metadata.series_title: Completed Class Tests and Examinations, Students’ Course Papers

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Coursework under
dispute may not be destroyed until
the resolution of the dispute.

series_metadata.series_title: Curriculum Files, Including Revisions, Central Administrative

tracking_data.comments:
Review before disposal as some
may merit permanent retention for
historical reasons.

Review before disposal as some
may merit permanent retention for
historical reasons.

series_metadata.series_description: Grade sheets submitted by instructors, advanced credit posting authorizations, and grade rolls or similar input documentation used in posting grades or credit data to transcripts

This series documents students' evaluations of non-tenure track teaching personnel and is used to help determine faculty promotion, merit increases and/or to review instructional courses and programs. These records provide students' opinions on faculty members' familiarity with current literature of the discipline, preparation, assignments, examinations, lecture styles, willingness to engage in dialogue, and availability. Records include: bubble forms (input documents); course reaction; inventory printouts; statistical tabulation; summary reports; and related documentation and correspondence.

This series documents students' evaluations of tenure track teaching personnel and is used to help determine faculty promotion, merit increases and/or to review instructional courses and programs. These records provide students' opinions on faculty members' familiarity with current literature of the discipline, preparation, assignments, examinations, lecture styles, willingness to engage in dialogue, and availability. Records include: bubble forms (input documents); course reaction; inventory printouts; statistical tabulation; summary reports; and related documentation and correspondence.

series_metadata.series_title: Leave Forms and Travel Files, Faculty
New Course Proposals File, Central Administrative

tracking_data.comments:
Note: Review before disposal as
some may merit permanent
retention for historical reasons.

Note: Review before disposal as
some may merit permanent
retention for historical reasons.

series_metadata.series_title: Software Programs and Job Control Language
series_metadata.series_description: Automated software applications and operating system files including job control language, program listing/source code, etc.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting operational and maintenance requirements of computer hardware such as operating manuals, hardware/operating system requirements, hardware configurations, and equipment control systems.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_metadata.series_description: Records adequate to specify all technical characteristics necessary for reading or processing of electronic records and their timely, authorized disposition including documentation describing how a system operates and necessary for using the system such as user guides, system or sub- system definitions, system specifications, input and output specifications, and system flow charts; program descriptions and documentation such as program flowcharts, program maintenance logs, change notices, and other records that document modifications to computer programs; and data documentation necessary to access, retrieve, manipulate and interpret data in an automated system such as a data element dictionary, file layout, code book or table, and other records that explain the meaning, purpose, structure, logical relationships, and origin of the data elements.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_metadata.series_description: Files needed for electronic data audits such as those showing transactions accepted, rejected, suspended, and/or processed; history files/tapes; records of on-line updates to application files or security logs.

series_metadata.series_title: Finding Aids, Indexes and Tracking Systems
series_metadata.series_description: Automated indexes, lists, registers and other finding aids used to provide access to the hard copy and electronic records.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and
archival code of the records they
support.

series_metadata.series_description: Electronic files or automated logs created to monitor computer systems such as print spool logs, tape activity logs, etc.

series_metadata.series_title: Chargeback Records to Data Processing Services Users
series_metadata.series_description: Records used to document, calculate costs, and bill program units for computer usage and data processing services. These records are also used for cost recovery, budgeting, or administrative purposes.
Schedules or similar records showing computer jobs to be run and other reports by computer operators or programmers of work performed.

tracking_data.comments:
Note: These may be kept longer if
they possess additional
administrative value.

series_metadata.series_description: Manuals, guidelines or similar documents establishing data processing policies and procedures in an agency in such areas as access and security, systems development, data retention and disposition, data ownership, production control, system back-up, etc.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
technical documentation of
procedures necessary for reading
or processing of electronic records.
SEE item number 2.1.009.

series_metadata.series_description: Forms and logs used to reconcile batches submitted for processing against batches received and processed.

series_metadata.series_title: Output Records for Computer Production (Mini and Mainframe)
series_metadata.series_description: Reports showing transactions that were accepted, rejected, suspended, and/or processed.

tracking_data.comments:
Output may be either in printed or
magnetic form. If magnetic, the
files, programs and documentation
must be available.

series_metadata.series_description: I nformation verifying the quality of system, hardware or software operations including records of errors or failures and the loss of data resulting from such failures, documentation of abnormal termination and of error free processing, checks of changes put into production, transaction histories, and other records needed as an audit trail to evaluate data accuracy.

Data resident on hard drives that make use of user-specific information transmitted by the Web server onto the user’s computer so that the information might be available for later access by itself or other servers.

tracking_data.comments:
The disposal of Internet cookies
need not be documented through
destruction authorizations
(1.2.001) or in records disposition
logs (1.2.010), but agencies should
establish procedures governing
disposal of these records as part of
its records management plan
(1.2.014).

series_metadata.series_description: A record of the documents visited during an Internet session that allows users to access previously visited pages more quickly or to generate a record of usage of a state-owned computer.

tracking_data.comments:
The disposal of History Files need
not be documented through
destruction authorizations
(1.2.001) or in records disposition
logs (1.2.010), but agencies should
establish procedures governing
disposal of these records as part of
its records management plan
(1.2.014).

series_metadata.series_title: Software Registrations, Warranties and License Agreements

series_metadata.series_description: Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of reference, and similar documents whose submission by candidates for vacant positions is required on the application form, by application procedures, or in the employment advertisement.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
41 CFR 60-300.44
and 41 CFR 60-
741.44 (Federal
Affirmative Action,
Veterans); 29 CFR
1602.31 (State
Agencies); 29 CFR
1602.49(a) (State
Universities)

series_metadata.series_description: Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of reference, and similar documents whose submission by candidates for vacant positions is required on the application form, by application procedures, or in the employment advertisement.
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to the counseling of an employee for work- related, personal, or substance abuse problems.
Copies of information relating to the selection by employees of life, disability, health, and other types of insurance.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorization
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number 3.2.001.

series_metadata.series_description: Internal or external announcements or advertisements of job openings, promotions, training programs, or opportunities for overtime.

Includes notes of interviews with candidates, questions asked of applicants, audio and videotapes of job interviews, driving records, and previous injury checks, pre-employment physical examinations, pre-employment drug testing, polygraph examination results; and all other records that document the selection process.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
41 CFR 60-300.44
and 41 CFR 60-
741.44 (Federal
Affirmative Action,
Veterans); 29 CFR
1602.31(State
Agencies), 29 CFR
1602.49(a) and Texas
Government Code
§411.094(e) (State
Universities)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
criminal history checks. SEE item
number 3.1.026.

series_metadata.series_description: Records relating to the review of employee grievances against personnel policies, working conditions, etc.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an employee
with the Equal Employment
Opportunity Commission (EEOC) of
the U.S. Department of Labor. See
Item Number 1.1.048.

series_metadata.series_description: Disciplinary actions are those actions which may affect pay, status, or tenure.

Forms or similar records used to create or change information concerning the records of an employee including pay grade, position classification, employee number, evaluation date, and termination of employment.

series_metadata.series_title: Position/Job Descriptions (for individuals; classified and nonclassified positions)
series_metadata.series_description: Job descriptions, including all associated task or skill statements.
Medical or physical examination reports or certificates of employees for whom periodic monitoring of health or fitness is required. (not to include pre-employment physicals)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include pre-
employment physical examinations.
SEE item 3.1.014.

series_metadata.series_description: Criminal history record information on job applicants or agency employees obtained from the Department of Public Safety (DPS).

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency’s
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_metadata.series_description: Certificates of completion, transcripts, test scores, or similar records documenting the training, testing, or continuing education achievements of an employee.

series_metadata.series_title: Documentation or Verification of Employment Eligibility
series_metadata.series_description: Federal reporting form (INS I-9)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Federal regulation
requires that INS I-9 forms be
retained for 3 years from date of
hire or 1 year after separation of
the employee, whichever is later.
Agencies should make certain that
the INS I-9 forms for employees
who terminate from an agency less
than 3 years from date of hire are
kept for the 3 year retention
period.

series_metadata.series_description: Agency copies of information relating to the selection of available benefit options other than insurance.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number 3.2.001.

series_metadata.series_description: Retention period applies if replies are made to the sender of resume stating that it will be kept on file for future job openings.

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 3.1.014 for
resumes, whether solicited or
unsolicited, that are used in any
way in the employment selection
process.

series_metadata.series_title: Performance Bonds / Surety Bonds (Executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015)
series_metadata.series_description: Bonds posted by employees and individuals or entities under contract with an institution for the performance of the duties of a position or the terms of a contract with the institution. a) Executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
construction or architectural surety
bonds. SEE item numbers 5.2.028
Building Construction Contracts
and 5.1.001 Contracts and Leases.

series_metadata.series_title: Performance Bonds / Surety Bonds (Executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015)
series_metadata.series_description: Bonds posted by employees and individuals or entities under contract with an institution for the performance of the duties of a position or the terms of a contract with the institution. b) Executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
construction or architectural surety
bonds. SEE item numbers 5.2.028
Building Construction Contracts
and 5.1.001 Contracts and Leases.

series_metadata.series_description: Summary of applicant qualifications, evaluation basis for selection or rejection, original applications, job assignments, promotions, separations, layoffs, terminations, compensation records, conditions of work, time records, hours of training provided (separate from labor), minority status, and gender of all selected and rejected applicants.
Awards, incentives, tenure, etc. (A copy of such letter/notice should be maintained in the employee's individual personnel file.)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include full
faculty promotion/tenure review
files. See 3.1.143.

series_metadata.series_description: F orm completed and signed by employee or official, or former employee or official, electing to keep home address, home telephone number, social security number, and family information open or confidential under the Public Information Act, Government Code 552.024.

tracking_data.comments: SEE item number 3.3.011

series_metadata.series_description: Consultation records, notes, letters, memos, emails, reports and other documentation.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaint filed with the EEOC or
other governmental agency. If
matter becomes a grievance or the
subject of counseling or litigation,
or employee is subject to
disciplinary action, the records are
subject to retention in the
appropriate records series. SEE
1.1.048, 3.1.006, 3.1.018, 3.1.020,
and 3.1.021.

series_metadata.series_title: Faculty Promotion and Tenure Review Records (Promotion File)
series_metadata.series_description: Evaluations, recommendations, and similar documentation relating to the review process for promotion or tenure for all faculty or staff in tenure track positions.

tracking_data.comments:
JC3850-06. NOTE: A copy of the
letter granting or denying
promotion/tenure, and any final
determination letter regarding an
appeal, should be maintained in the
employee's individual personnel file
in accordance with 3.1.137 and
3.3.135, 3.3.136 or 3.3.137.

series_metadata.series_description: Documentation used to start, modify, or stop all voluntary or required deductions from payroll, including garnishment or other court-ordered attachments.
Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax records.

tracking_data.comments:
(Retention is based from return,
paid or claim date)

series_metadata.series_description: Used to make increases or decreases to employees’ gross pay, FICA, retirement, or in the computation of taxes.
Employer’s copy of “Employee’s Withholding Exemption Certificate.”

tracking_data.comments:
For instruction regarding the
determination of the closure of
accounts and for additional
information regarding the retention
period see the most current edition
of the Benefits Coordinator
Reference Manual issued by the
Employees Retirement System of
Texas.

series_metadata.series_description: Includes supporting documentation.
For both regular employees and apprenticeship programs
Employee benefit plans such as pension, life, health and disability insurance, deferred compensation, etc. including amendments.
Reports providing statistical information on labor force.
Minimum information needed to verify employment, includes name, social security number, exact dates of employment, last known address and most recent public access option form (3.1.038).
Records relating to review and monitoring of job classifications within an agency.
Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules, rosters or assignments

series_metadata.series_title: Texas Workforce Commission (TWC) Reports (from TWC to agency)
Reimbursable Activities, Requests and Authorizations to Engage in
series_metadata.series_description: Requests and authorizations for travel; participation in educational programs, workshops, or college classes, or for other work-related activities for which the expenses of the employee are defrayed or reimbursed.
Any internally distributed manuals, guidelines, or similar records that define agency wide policies and procedures concerning the personnel of an agency.
Any documents detailing the procedural duties and responsibilities of agency positions on a position-by-position basis.

Any report compiled by an agency on aspects of personnel staffing, including listings of all staff by program or name, staff hired during a month, detailed listings of employees within its organizational structure, position vacancies, analysis of turnover rates and seasonality of employment, etc.
Aptitude or skills tests required of job applicants or of current personnel to qualify for promotion or transfer.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each
different test (different in terms of
either questions or administration
procedures) should be retained for
the period indicated.

series_metadata.series_description: Aptitude or skills test papers job applicants or of current personnel taking a test to qualify for promotion or transfer.
Records of the validation of aptitude and skills tests.

Instructional materials and other records associated with in-house training of agency personnel on personnel policies and procedures and other policies and procedures that govern an agency’s programs, services or projects.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
hazardous material training
records. SEE item number 5.4.007.

series_metadata.series_description: Includes documentation used to complete EEO reports.
Reports, studies, aggregated or summary data, and similar documentation compiled to monitor and demonstrate compliance with the federal Equal Pay Act.

tracking_data.comments:
